claim: PKG-la plays an essential role in expression of pain hypersensitivity in PGK-la knockout mice.

result.claim: PKG-la plays an essential role in expression of pain hypersensitivity in PGK-la knockout mice.
result.reasoning: The claim is strongly supported by evidence from paper ID 11254556, "Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dependent Protein Kinase 1 Is a Key Determinant of Spinal Synaptic Potentiation and Pain Hypersensitivity". This paper directly investigates the role of Protein Kinase G1 (PKG-I), which is referred to as PKG-la in the claim, in pain hypersensitivity using knockout mice. The abstract and several propositions from this paper explicitly state that PKG-I plays an essential role in the expression of spinal LTP and that SNS-PKG-I(-/-) (nociceptor-specific PKG-I knockout) mice showed marked defects in models of activity-induced nociceptive hypersensitivity. Specifically, proposition 1 states that "SNS-PKG-I(-/-) mice showed marked defects in several models of activity-induced nociceptive hypersensitivity," and proposition 3 indicates that "PKG-I, expressed in spinal terminals of nociceptors, contributes to pain sensitivity." The paper also details the generation of these knockout mice (proposition 5) and the presynaptic localization of PKG-I in nociceptor terminals (proposition 6). While the paper has 0 citations and an unknown publication year, the direct and consistent nature of the findings within the abstract and propositions provides strong support for the claim.

evidence.text: Activity-induced LTP at identified synapses between nociceptors and spinal neurons projecting to the periaqueductal grey (PAG) was completely abolished in SNS-PKG-I(-/-) mice.
evidence.source: Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dependent Protein Kinase 1 Is a Key Determinant of Spinal Synaptic Potentiation and Pain Hypersensitivity

evidence.text: Researchers generated nociceptor-specific knockout mice lacking PKG-I specifically in presynaptic terminals of nociceptors in the spinal cord.
evidence.source: Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dependent Protein Kinase 1 Is a Key Determinant of Spinal Synaptic Potentiation and Pain Hypersensitivity

evidence.text: Inositol 1,4,5-triphosphate receptor 1 and myosin light chain kinase were recruited as key phosphorylation targets of presynaptic PKG-I in nociceptive neurons.
evidence.source: Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dependent Protein Kinase 1 Is a Key Determinant of Spinal Synaptic Potentiation and Pain Hypersensitivity

evidence.text: The interaction between nociceptors and spinal neurons projecting to the periaqueductal grey (PAG) was completely abolished in SNS-PKG-I(-/-) mice.
evidence.source: Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dependent Protein Kinase 1 Is a Key Determinant of Spinal Synaptic Potentiation and Pain Hypersensitivity

evidence.text: Loss of PKCζ in mice results in enhanced intestinal tumorigenesis.
evidence.source: Control of Nutrient Stress-Induced Metabolic Reprogramming by PKCζ in Tumorigenesis

evidence.text: The gp130F759/F759 mice showed prolonged gp130-induced STAT3 activation.
evidence.source: Dissection of signaling cascades through gp130 in vivo: reciprocal roles for STAT3- and SHP2-mediated signals in immune responses.

evidence.text: Genetic ablation of prostaglandin E synthases in Braf(V600E) mouse melanoma cells renders them susceptible to immune control.
evidence.source: Cyclooxygenase-Dependent Tumor Growth through Evasion of Immunity

evidence.text: The gp130F759/F759 mice showed an enhanced acute phase reaction.
evidence.source: Dissection of signaling cascades through gp130 in vivo: reciprocal roles for STAT3- and SHP2-mediated signals in immune responses.

evidence.text: Protein Kinase G1 (PKG-I) localized presynaptically in nociceptor terminals.
evidence.source: Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dependent Protein Kinase 1 Is a Key Determinant of Spinal Synaptic Potentiation and Pain Hypersensitivity

evidence.text: In an experimental mouse model of lung fibrosis, AMPK activity is lower in fibrotic regions.
evidence.source: Metformin reverses established lung fibrosis in a bleomycin model

evidence.text: Basal synaptic transmission was not affected in SNS-PKG-I(-/-) mice.
